The Tier-3 fidelity work landed alongside. ~bf18aa3~ landed T1.1, T1.2, T1.3 as the first wave. ~93fdf0d~ added the runtime slack adjustment from Ipopt's ~slack_move~ logic. ~81f2f83~ ported the ~RestoreAcceptablePoint~ mechanism — Ipopt's last-resort revert to the best feasible iterate seen so far, when the filter has eaten the recent history and the solver is otherwise out of options. ~c0dfd57~ landed T2.21, the watchdog filter fidelity and tiny-step exit from §4 of the spec. ~8ca29fc~ landed T2.22, restoration NLP signs and ~kappa_resto~ acceptance from §7. ~d7639aa~ batched T3.12, T3.23, T3.25 and the Tier-3 audit doc. ~8f6a129~, A8.7, hoisted the ~aug_solver~ above the IPM loop so feral's symbolic-cache cost was paid once per solve instead of once per iteration. ~55872ee~, A7.8, wired the sparse augmented factor in past a size threshold so small problems still went down the dense Bunch-Kaufman path.

The augmented (4-block) KKT port — variables, slacks, equality multipliers, inequality multipliers each in their own block, with the sparsity exploited natively rather than collapsed into the dense ~M~-block — was the architectural prerequisite for everything else. By A7.9, on April 29, the audit was as close to complete as it was going to get this cycle: HS 113/120, CUTEst 555/727 (one off Ipopt's 561), electrolyte 13/13, large-scale 10/10, all 295 library tests green. Track C was done except for one deferred item that needed a ~LinearSolver~ trait revision. Eleven roadmap items had landed in v0.7.1; the v0.8 audit had landed dozens more, most of them invisible at the benchmark level because they were correctness fixes for cases the benchmark suite did not exercise.

** A8 — The Problem That Will Not Solve

The only ~in_progress~ task that survived the audit closeout was task #24: /Verify with arki0003 and adversary suite./ arki0003 was a Mittelmann benchmark with ~n = 1872~, ~m = 2138~ (447 equality, 1691 inequality), 1041 linear constraints, 8515 inequality-Jacobian non-zeros, 2037 Hessian non-zeros. Ipopt solves it. ripopt did not.

~docs/A8_FOLLOWUP_arki0003.md~, opened on April 29, became the working document for the rest of the cycle. The reproduction was a single command:

#+begin_example
timeout 600 ./target/release/ripopt benchmarks/mittelmann/nl/arki0003.nl \
    -AMPL max_iter=3000
#+end_example

The failure mode, from the iteration log, was structured. Iters 0-10 had the bound-push clipping the initial point's $|x|_\infty = 10^7$ into the box, with step sizes collapsing to $\alpha \sim 10^{-4}$ and the filter rejecting everything except infinitesimal moves. Iters 11-50 were /jumping around/ — multi-decade swings in the objective, $-5.4 \times 10^4 \to -3.5 \times 10^3 \to +1.7 \times 10^4 \to -3.1 \times 10^6 \to -1.0 \times 10^7 \to +8.6 \times 10^5 \to +4.0 \times 10^6$, before the filter and the second-order correction finally tamed the iterate around $4$ - $6 \times 10^3$. Iters 76-217 were real descent — objective down to $3.83 \times 10^3$, $\inf_{pr}$ down to $10^{-7}$, $\mu$ down from $10^{-1}$ to $3 \times 10^{-3}$. And then, from around iter 600 onward, hard dual stagnation: same iterate every step, $\alpha_{pr} = \alpha_{du} = 1.0$ every iteration, $\inf_{du} = 6.54 \times 10^6$ frozen, ~compl~ stuck at $0.15$, the step being computed and scored and applied, and the iterate not moving.

A8 ran from May 1 through May 6 as a numbered sequence of probes. ~A8.9~, ~303243a~, dropped the ~apply_damped_y_update~ sign-flip heuristic — a v0.6.x band-aid Ipopt did not have. ~A8.10~, ~67843a8~, dropped ~kappa_d~ from the ~inf_du~ metric to match Ipopt. ~A8.11~ went through three sub-attempts: ~3f4c82d~ dropped the ~barrier_subproblem_solved~ gate from the Free-mode μ update; ~fe111d4~ flipped the default ~mu_strategy~ to ~monotone~ to match Ipopt 3.14; ~56664e1~ recorded a reverted attempt and the oracle-safety follow-up. ~A8.12~, ~be043df~, aligned ~DetectTinyStep~ with Ipopt 3.14. ~A8.13~, ~b79fd27~, gated the stall-μ-boost on the adaptive strategy. ~A8.14~, ~b2ca586~, aligned ~compute_barrier_error~ with Ipopt's ~curr_barrier_error~. ~A8.15~, the re-measurement, recorded that none of DEV-1 through DEV-23 had moved arki0003.

By A8.18, ~5647a33~ had identified the divergence point: arki0003's iter 110 → 111 was where ripopt and Ipopt parted company. A8.19, ~cc4c298~, aligned filter $\theta$ with Ipopt's slack-coupling. A8.20, ~ea25336~, recorded that the apparent convergence at iter 110 had been illusory — ripopt was reporting a small ~compl~ that Ipopt's check would have flagged as insufficient. A8.21, ~f15a12c~, was an iter-0 ~dx~ probe that ruled out feral as the root cause: the very first Newton step out of the initial point was numerically identical to what Ipopt computed, so the linear solver was not the culprit. A8.22, ~5bd9953~, ruled out a scaling-propagation hypothesis. A8.23, ~540d314~, recorded the post-P1 through P10 re-measurement: still walls out.

The diagnosis had narrowed to two candidates. The first was the perturbation handler — ~PDPerturbationHandler~ (T3.27) — committing a large $\delta_x$ in a previous iteration after a ~Degenerate~ lock, with ripopt's ~_last~ field carrying it forward instead of resetting on a ~SUCCESS~ no-correction factor. The second was the post-restoration handoff: when restoration returned with a feasible iterate, the multipliers being passed back to the main IPM did not match the convention Ipopt's ~RestoIpoptNLP~ used. ~41186c2~, /fix(restoration): Ipopt-align post-restoration handoff (y, z_L, z_U, v_L, v_U)/, ported the synthetic Newton step $\delta_z = (\mu - z_{\text{curr}} \cdot s_{\text{trial}})/s_{\text{curr}} - z_{\text{curr}}$ for all four blocks. ~538fd83~, /fix(restoration): use internal-max primal infeasibility for resto_mu (gap A)/, fixed the ~resto_mu~ initialization to use the internal-max primal infeasibility instead of the post-scaling value. ~8a509d1~, the one-shot post-restoration ~recalc_y~ override, was applied; ~2fa508c~ reverted it the same day after a regression on a different problem. ~c72802b~ aligned ~Compare_le~ tolerance and the restoration bypass with Ipopt 3.14. ~acf87f5~ aligned ~AugmentFilter~ and the ~Acceptable~ convention.

~074e129~, on May 4, added three diagnostic gates — ~RIPOPT_RHS_PROBE~, ~FILTER_DUMP~, ~DX_PROBE~ — for arki0003 forward-walk, so the next session could resume the search without re-instrumenting the IPM. ~e8dff73~ added ~RIPOPT_TRACE_MU~ and ~RIPOPT_TRACE_COMPL~ for the μ-update root-cause work. The diagnostic gates are still in the tree. They will be needed.

arki0003 still does not solve.

** What Did Move

A lot, just not the named target. CUTEst climbed from 555/727 at A7.9 to 564/727 at v0.8.0 — three /more/ than Ipopt's 561, and eight more than ripopt's own v0.7.1 number of 556. The shift was redistribution: 20 problems where ripopt now exclusively succeeds and Ipopt fails, 17 the other way. The geometric mean speedup on the 544 commonly-solved problems was 8.9× — down from v0.7.1's 12.0×, partly because feral's GEMM throughput on the largest sparse fronts has not yet matched MUMPS's, partly because the augmented 4-block path costs more per iteration than the collapsed 2-block one used to. The HS suite went /down/ to 113/120 from 118/120; the loss was concentrated on least-squares problems where the v0.8 stricter convergence test now correctly flags iterates the v0.7.1 looser check had been declaring ~Optimal~ on. Honest signal again, and a regression the CHANGELOG records as such.

The failure profile shifted shape entirely. v0.7.1's CUTEst tail had been dominated by ~NumericalError~ at 76 cases. v0.8's tail is dominated by ~RestorationFailed~ at 74 cases, ~MaxIterations~ at 39, ~LocalInfeasibility~ at 27, ~NumericalError~ at 14. The reclassification is what the alignment work bought: iterates that v0.7.1 would have flagged as numerical breakdown are now correctly identified as restoration-stalled, because the post-restoration handoff is right and the convergence test no longer accepts iterates that should be running further. arki0003 is in the ~MaxIterations~ pile of that count. So is the rest of the dual-stagnation cohort it is the named representative of.

The v0.8.0 release shipped on May 6 with ~fbb1f73~ as the version bump. The manuscript was refreshed on the same day (~2ff080c~) and refreshed again the day after (~775d4d6~) when a second-pass review caught stale v0.7.1 numbers in the Speed-vs-Accuracy section, the Opportunities for Improvement intro, the impact table, the Conclusions, and the AC OPF subsection. The manuscript abstract now reads /On the CUTEst benchmark suite of 727 problems at v0.8.0, ripopt strictly solves 564 (77.6%) versus Ipopt's 561 (77.2%)./ The concluding paragraph reads /the failure analysis reveals that =RestorationFailed= is now the dominant failure mode on CUTEst (74 cases, 45.4%), followed by =MaxIterations= (39, 23.9%) and =LocalInfeasibility= (27, 16.6%). The shift from v0.7.1's =NumericalError=-dominated profile reflects the v0.8 Ipopt-aligned post-restoration handoff correctly classifying iterates as restoration-stalled rather than as numerical breakdown---an honest signal where the solver is actually getting stuck./

What is /not/ in the manuscript, and is in the commit log and the A8 follow-up, is the unsolved problem. arki0003 is the public marker for a class of failures the v0.8 work narrowed but did not close: dual stagnation under the augmented KKT path, after restoration has handed back a feasible iterate, with the perturbation handler quite possibly carrying a stale $\delta_x$ across an iteration boundary it should have reset on. The diagnostic gates are wired. The hypothesis is two-deep. The next session has the entry points it needs.

** The Shape, Once More

Chapter Eight: /narrow the gap./ Chapter Nine: /and publish where the gap still is./ Chapter Ten: /and work the published list, in order, with one commit per change and one measurement per commit./ Chapter Eleven: /and decouple the parts of the stack that were entangled enough to make the alignment unauditable, even when the alignment work alone could not have closed every gap./

v0.8 moved the sparse linear solver out of the ripopt tree and into an independently developed, independently benchmarked sister project; climbed three problems past Ipopt on aggregate CUTEst pass rate without regressing correctness on any commonly-solved problem; reclassified the failure tail from /numerical error/ to /restoration stalled/ in a way that is honest rather than cosmetic; shrank ~src/ipm.rs~ from a single-author monolith to a navigable module; and brought feral to its first crates.io release in the process. It did not solve arki0003. It also did not pretend to.

The question, again, has moved. v0.8 was /can we extract the linear solver into a separately benchmarked component, align the IPM kernel with the Ipopt 3.14 reference, and have the failure tail tell the truth about where each layer is stuck?/ The answer is yes, mostly. arki0003 is the part of the answer that is /not yet, and we now know which layer it is not./
